what_it_does: A ledger of extracted visual/design atoms (components) observed across pitch-deck and landing-page substrates, each annotated with a recipe, evidence slides, generator rule, source deck, and compression depth.
observations: The file is a JSON array where every object has category 'atom' and describes one visual component.
Many entries describe the same broad concept repeatedly with slightly different measurements, e.g., the bottom-right V badge, stat cards, QR overlays, and gold triangle bullets.
Evidence is given as slide/page references such as 'p-02', 'p16', 'lp2 strip_02', etc.
Some atoms are flagged 'emergent': true, while structurally similar recurring atoms are not.
Color values vary across entries, e.g., #C8A84B, #C9A227, #C4A832, #C8960A, #C8A020, #C9A84C, #E0A82C.
The file mixes deck components and web landing-page components in one array.
standouts: Gold right-pointing triangle as the universal list bullet across almost every substrate.
Persistent V corner mark/watermark on every content slide, treated as a non-optional brand signature.
Stat card as the canonical data atom: gold number + small descriptor in a bordered cream rectangle.
QR code overlay as an interactive portal embedded in photo contexts.
Line-art icon system used as a complete row of industry-stage icons.
conventions: Each entry is named with a pattern like 'X — Y' and category is always 'atom'.
The 'recipe' field gives visual specs: size, color, position, stroke, fill, radius, padding.
The 'evidence' field cites slide or page IDs where the atom was observed.
The 'generator_rule' field states a prescriptive rule for reproducing the atom.
_src and _depth record provenance and the compression level of the source substrate.
Gold is the dominant accent color; warm cream/off-white is the dominant fill.
Rounded rectangles, thin borders, and equal internal padding recur throughout.
Right-pointing arrows/triangles encode forward motion; left-pointing arrows encode inbound results.
concerns: Conflicting numeric values for the same atom across sources, e.g., V-badge diameter ranges from 20px to 36px.
Duplicate entries for nearly identical concepts may complicate downstream fusion.
Some generator rules are imperative but rest on sparse evidence (e.g., a single slide).
The 'emergent' flag is applied inconsistently to similar recurring atoms.
Color codes are not normalized, making tokenization harder.
Slide/page evidence IDs are file-local and not mapped to any global page model.
notes: A design-system audit trail: every entry is a prescriptive atom extracted from a specific substrate depth, intended to feed a fused component grammar.
questions: What is the intended granularity boundary between an atom and a larger molecule?
How should conflicting measurements for the same component be resolved during fusion?
Is '_depth' a controlled vocabulary, and if so, what is the full set of allowed values?
Why are some recurring atoms marked 'emergent' while others are not?
Are color values expected to be normalized into a shared token set?
What is the relationship between the different '_src' decks and landing pages?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is a findings ledger for extracted design components, which matches its content; it catalogs atoms with recipes and rules rather than raw observations only.
intention_signals: To turn visual impressions from multiple pitch and page substrates into reusable, prescriptive generator rules for a unified design language.
novelty: The explicit pairing of a descriptive 'recipe' with a prescriptive 'generator_rule' and provenance metadata (_src/_depth) for each visual atom, treating design components as versioned, compression-dependent findings.
contribution: Provides a structured inventory of observed visual atoms with source and depth context, giving a fused system concrete component recipes, evidence references, and prescriptive rules to reconcile.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON array of design atoms extracted from pitch decks and landing pages, each documenting a visual component's recipe, evidence, generator rule, source substrate, and compression depth.
intention_for_embedding: To capture and codify recurring visual components across presentation and web substrates so they can be synthesized into a shared design-system grammar.
